breaking.title: West Asia war LIVE: Iran launches missiles at Israel for the first time since the April ceasefire
breaking.summary: Israel military chief vows to strike Iran 'with force as soon as greenlight is given'; Iran's Revolutionary Guards said that the missile attack on Israel was a "warning" after the Israeli strike on Beirut on Sunday

breaking.title: Gaza health officials say 10 killed in Israeli attacks
breaking.summary: In a separate incident, Israeli naval forces shot dead a fisherman in the early hours of Sunday, on June 7, the Al-Aqsa Hospital in Deir el-Balah said after receiving his body.

breaking.title: The Papers:  'Iran fires missile barrage' and 'New Eriksen hell'
breaking.summary: Iran's latest strike on Israel and Danish footballer Christian Eriksen's on-field collapse are prominent on Monday's papers.

breaking.title: Israel strikes Beirut suburb days after US-brokered truce
breaking.summary: Israel says the attacks on the Lebanese capital were ordered  "in response to Hezbollah's firing at Israeli territory".

breaking.title: Trump abruptly ends NBC interview after clash over 'rigged election' claim
breaking.summary: During the interview, the president was repeatedly challenged on several points by the show's presenter Kristen Welker.

breaking.title: 'No dead ends': What the Dutch can teach us about tackling youth unemployment
breaking.summary: The Netherlands has one of the world's lowest rates of 16 to 24-year-olds not in education, employment or training.

breaking.title: Iran’s top negotiator threatens U.S. targets over Lebanon escalation
breaking.summary: The comments from ​Iran’s ‌Parliament speaker, Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, follow Israeli attacks on the southern ‌suburbs of Lebanon’s capital, ​a stronghold of Iran’s ally ⁠Hezbollah.

breaking.title: U.K., France, Germany back direct Ukraine-Russia talks
breaking.summary: The leaders “supported the proposal for a direct dialogue between Ukraine and Russia - with active U.S. and European participation - to bring about a ceasefire and support further negotiations,” they said in a joint statement with Mr. Zelenskyy.
